message:
fsfs: default search to daemon transport on unix, add --no-daemon flag,
and gracefully fall back when daemon is unavailable

CLI adapter (cli.rs):
- Introduce a `daemon_preference: Option<bool>` variable to track
  whether the user explicitly passed --daemon or --no-daemon, deferring
  the final decision to a new `apply_daemon_settings()` function after
  all arguments are parsed.
- Add --no-daemon flag that explicitly disables daemon transport, which
  is useful for debugging or environments where the daemon socket is
  not available.
- Extend --daemon and --no-daemon validity to both Search and Serve
  commands (previously --daemon was restricted to Search only). The
  error messages are updated accordingly.
- `apply_daemon_settings()` implements the new defaulting logic:
  - If the user passed an explicit preference, honor it unconditionally.
  - On unix (cfg(unix)), default `search` commands to daemon=true unless
    --stream mode is active (streaming bypasses daemon transport).
  - On non-unix platforms, default daemon=false since unix domain
    sockets are not available.
- Register "--no-daemon" in `is_known_cli_flag()` so the unknown-flag
  diagnostic does not fire.
- Add unit tests:
  - `parse_serve_accepts_daemon_flag`: validates --daemon works with
    the serve command.
  - `parse_search_defaults_to_daemon_on_unix`: confirms auto-daemon
    on unix, auto-off on non-unix.
  - `parse_no_daemon_disables_auto_daemon_transport`: confirms explicit
    --no-daemon overrides the unix default.
  - `parse_no_daemon_rejects_non_search_or_serve_command`: confirms
    --no-daemon is rejected for status/index/etc.
  - Updated existing `parse_stream_flag` test to assert daemon=false
    when streaming.
  - Renamed `parse_daemon_rejects_non_search_command` to
    `parse_daemon_rejects_non_search_or_serve_command` and updated the
    expected error message.

Runtime (runtime.rs):
- In the search dispatch path, wrap the daemon call in a match block:
  if `search_payloads_via_daemon()` returns an error, log a warning
  with the error details and transparently fall back to in-process
  `execute_search_payloads_cached_for_cli()`. This prevents daemon
  socket failures (e.g., daemon not running, stale socket) from
  becoming hard errors for the user.
- In `run_search_serve_command()`, check `self.cli_input.daemon` in
  addition to `daemon_socket.is_some()` when deciding whether to use
  socket-based serving. This allows `fsfs serve --daemon` to activate
  socket mode without requiring an explicit --daemon-socket path.
- Update the help text:
  - search command description notes "(daemon-backed by default on unix)"
  - serve command description notes "(stdio by default, socket daemon
    with --daemon)"
  - Add a "Search flags:" line listing --daemon, --no-daemon,
    --daemon-socket, and --stream.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
